packages.name: aead
packages.description:
Traits for Authenticated Encryption with Associated Data (AEAD) algorithms,
such as AES-GCM as ChaCha20Poly1305, which provide a high-level API

packages.name: aho-corasick
packages.description: Fast multiple substring searching.

packages.name: arrayvec
packages.description: A vector with fixed capacity, backed by an array (it can be stored on the stack too). Implements fixed capacity ArrayVec and ArrayString.

packages.name: atty
packages.description: A simple interface for querying atty

packages.name: autocfg
packages.description: Automatic cfg for Rust compiler features

packages.name: base64
packages.description: encodes and decodes base64 as bytes or utf8

packages.name: bitflags
packages.description: A macro to generate structures which behave like bitflags.

packages.name: bitflags
packages.description: A macro to generate structures which behave like bitflags.

packages.name: blake2
packages.description: BLAKE2 hash functions

packages.name: block-buffer
packages.description: Buffer type for block processing of data

packages.name: bumpalo
packages.description: A fast bump allocation arena for Rust.

packages.name: cast
packages.description: Ergonomic, checked cast functions for primitive types

packages.name: cc
packages.description:
A build-time dependency for Cargo build scripts to assist in invoking the native
C compiler to compile native C code into a static archive to be linked into Rust
code.

packages.name: cfg-if
packages.description:
A macro to ergonomically define an item depending on a large number of #[cfg]
parameters. Structured like an if-else chain, the first matching branch is the
item that gets emitted.

packages.name: chacha20
packages.description:
The ChaCha20 stream cipher (RFC 8439) implemented in pure Rust using traits
from the RustCrypto `cipher` crate, with optional architecture-specific
hardware acceleration (AVX2, SSE2). Additionally provides the ChaCha8, ChaCha12,
XChaCha20, XChaCha12 and XChaCha8 stream ciphers, and also optional
rand_core-compatible RNGs based on those ciphers.

packages.name: chacha20poly1305
packages.description:
Pure Rust implementation of the ChaCha20Poly1305 Authenticated Encryption
with Additional Data Cipher (RFC 8439) with optional architecture-specific
hardware acceleration. Also contains implementations of the XChaCha20Poly1305
extended nonce variant of ChaCha20Poly1305, and the reduced-round
ChaCha8Poly1305 and ChaCha12Poly1305 lightweight variants.

packages.name: cipher
packages.description: Traits for describing block ciphers and stream ciphers

packages.name: clap
packages.description: A simple to use, efficient, and full-featured Command Line Argument Parser

packages.name: convert_case
packages.description: Convert strings into any case

packages.name: cpufeatures
packages.description:
Lightweight runtime CPU feature detection for aarch64, loongarch64, and x86/x86_64 targets, 
with no_std support and support for mobile targets including Android and iOS

packages.name: criterion
packages.description: Statistics-driven micro-benchmarking library

packages.name: criterion-plot
packages.description: Criterion's plotting library

packages.name: crossbeam-deque
packages.description: Concurrent work-stealing deque

packages.name: crossbeam-epoch
packages.description: Epoch-based garbage collection

packages.name: crossbeam-utils
packages.description: Utilities for concurrent programming

packages.name: crypto-common
packages.description: Common cryptographic traits

packages.name: csv
packages.description: Fast CSV parsing with support for serde.

packages.name: csv-core
packages.description: Bare bones CSV parsing with no_std support.

packages.name: curve25519-dalek
packages.description: A pure-Rust implementation of group operations on ristretto255 and Curve25519

packages.name: curve25519-dalek-derive
packages.description: curve25519-dalek Derives

packages.name: derive_more
packages.description: Adds #[derive(x)] macros for more traits

packages.name: derive_more-impl
packages.description: Internal implementation of `derive_more` crate

packages.name: digest
packages.description: Traits for cryptographic hash functions and message authentication codes

packages.name: either
packages.description: The enum `Either` with variants `Left` and `Right` is a general purpose sum type with two cases.

packages.name: etherparse
packages.description: A library for parsing & writing a bunch of packet based protocols (EthernetII, IPv4, IPv6, UDP, TCP ...).

packages.name: fiat-crypto
packages.description: Fiat-crypto generated Rust

packages.name: find-msvc-tools
packages.description: Find windows-specific tools, read MSVC versions from the registry and from COM interfaces

packages.name: futures
packages.description:
An implementation of futures and streams featuring zero allocations,
composability, and iterator-like interfaces.

packages.name: futures-channel
packages.description: Channels for asynchronous communication using futures-rs.

packages.name: futures-core
packages.description: The core traits and types in for the `futures` library.

packages.name: futures-executor
packages.description: Executors for asynchronous tasks based on the futures-rs library.

packages.name: futures-io
packages.description: The `AsyncRead`, `AsyncWrite`, `AsyncSeek`, and `AsyncBufRead` traits for the futures-rs library.

packages.name: futures-macro
packages.description: The futures-rs procedural macro implementations.

packages.name: futures-sink
packages.description: The asynchronous `Sink` trait for the futures-rs library.

packages.name: futures-task
packages.description: Tools for working with tasks.

packages.name: futures-util
packages.description: Common utilities and extension traits for the futures-rs library.

packages.name: generic-array
packages.description: Generic types implementing functionality of arrays

packages.name: getrandom
packages.description: A small cross-platform library for retrieving random data from system source

packages.name: half
packages.description: Half-precision floating point f16 and bf16 types for Rust implementing the IEEE 754-2008 standard binary16 and bfloat16 types.

packages.name: hermit-abi
packages.description:
hermit-abi is small interface to call functions from the unikernel RustyHermit.
It is used to build the target `x86_64-unknown-hermit`.

packages.name: hex
packages.description: Encoding and decoding data into/from hexadecimal representation.

packages.name: hmac
packages.description: Generic implementation of Hash-based Message Authentication Code (HMAC)

packages.name: inout
packages.description: Custom reference types for code generic over in-place and buffer-to-buffer modes of operation.

packages.name: itertools
packages.description: Extra iterator adaptors, iterator methods, free functions, and macros.

packages.name: itoa
packages.description: Fast integer primitive to string conversion

packages.name: js-sys
packages.description:
Bindings for all JS global objects and functions in all JS environments like
Node.js and browsers, built on `#[wasm_bindgen]` using the `wasm-bindgen` crate.

packages.name: lazy_static
packages.description: A macro for declaring lazily evaluated statics in Rust.

packages.name: libc
packages.description: Raw FFI bindings to platform libraries like libc.

packages.name: lock_api
packages.description: Wrappers to create fully-featured Mutex and RwLock types. Compatible with no_std.

packages.name: log
packages.description: A lightweight logging facade for Rust

packages.name: memchr
packages.description:
Provides extremely fast (uses SIMD on x86_64, aarch64 and wasm32) routines for
1, 2 or 3 byte search and single substring search.

packages.name: nix
packages.description: Rust friendly bindings to *nix APIs

packages.name: nu-ansi-term
packages.description: Library for ANSI terminal colors and styles (bold, underline)

packages.name: num-traits

packages.name: once_cell
packages.description: Single assignment cells and lazy values.

packages.name: oorandom
packages.description: A tiny, robust PRNG implementation.

packages.name: opaque-debug
packages.description: Macro for opaque Debug trait implementation

packages.name: parking_lot
packages.description: More compact and efficient implementations of the standard synchronization primitives.

packages.name: parking_lot_core
packages.description: An advanced API for creating custom synchronization primitives.

packages.name: pin-project-lite
packages.description: A lightweight version of pin-project written with declarative macros.

packages.name: plotters
packages.description: A Rust drawing library focus on data plotting for both WASM and native applications

packages.name: plotters-backend
packages.description: Plotters Backend API

packages.name: plotters-svg
packages.description: Plotters SVG backend

packages.name: poly1305
packages.description: The Poly1305 universal hash function and message authentication code

packages.name: proc-macro2
packages.description: A substitute implementation of the compiler's `proc_macro` API to decouple token-based libraries from the procedural macro use case.

packages.name: proton-boringtun
packages.description: Fork of CloudFlare BoringTUN: an implementation of the WireGuard® protocol designed for portability and speed

packages.name: quote
packages.description: Quasi-quoting macro quote!(...)

packages.name: rand_core
packages.description: Core random number generator traits and tools for implementation.

packages.name: rand_core
packages.description: Core random number generator traits and tools for implementation.

packages.name: rayon
packages.description: Simple work-stealing parallelism for Rust

packages.name: rayon-core
packages.description: Core APIs for Rayon

packages.name: redox_syscall
packages.description: A Rust library to access raw Redox system calls

packages.name: regex
packages.description:
An implementation of regular expressions for Rust. This implementation uses
finite automata and guarantees linear time matching on all inputs.

packages.name: regex-automata
packages.description: Automata construction and matching using regular expressions.

packages.name: regex-syntax
packages.description: A regular expression parser.

packages.name: ring
packages.description: An experiment.

dependencies.target: cfg(all(all(target_arch = "aarch64", target_endian = "little"), target_vendor = "apple", any(target_os = "ios", target_os = "macos", target_os = "tvos", target_os = "visionos", target_os = "watchos")))

packages.name: rustc_version
packages.description: A library for querying the version of a installed rustc compiler

packages.name: rustversion
packages.description: Conditional compilation according to rustc compiler version

packages.name: ryu
packages.description: Fast floating point to string conversion

packages.name: same-file
packages.description: A simple crate for determining whether two file paths point to the same file.

packages.name: scopeguard
packages.description:
A RAII scope guard that will run a given closure when it goes out of scope,
even if the code between panics (assuming unwinding panic).

Defines the macros `defer!`, `defer_on_unwind!`, `defer_on_success!` as
shorthands for guards with one of the implemented strategies.

packages.name: semver
packages.description: Parser and evaluator for Cargo's flavor of Semantic Versioning

packages.name: serde
packages.description: A generic serialization/deserialization framework

packages.name: serde_cbor
packages.description: CBOR support for serde.

packages.name: serde_core
packages.description: Serde traits only, with no support for derive -- use the `serde` crate instead

packages.name: serde_derive
packages.description: Macros 1.1 implementation of #[derive(Serialize, Deserialize)]

packages.name: serde_json
packages.description: A JSON serialization file format

packages.name: sharded-slab
packages.description: A lock-free concurrent slab.

packages.name: shlex
packages.description: Split a string into shell words, like Python's shlex.

packages.name: slab
packages.description: Pre-allocated storage for a uniform data type

packages.name: smallvec
packages.description: 'Small vector' optimization: store up to a small number of items on the stack

packages.name: subtle
packages.description: Pure-Rust traits and utilities for constant-time cryptographic implementations.

packages.name: syn
packages.description: Parser for Rust source code

packages.name: textwrap
packages.description:
Textwrap is a small library for word wrapping, indenting, and
dedenting strings.

You can use it to format strings (such as help and error messages) for
display in commandline applications. It is designed to be efficient
and handle Unicode characters correctly.

packages.name: thiserror
packages.description: derive(Error)

packages.name: thiserror-impl
packages.description: Implementation detail of the `thiserror` crate

packages.name: thread_local
packages.description: Per-object thread-local storage

packages.name: tinytemplate
packages.description: Simple, lightweight template engine

packages.name: tracing
packages.description: Application-level tracing for Rust.

packages.name: tracing-attributes
packages.description: Procedural macro attributes for automatically instrumenting functions.

packages.name: tracing-core
packages.description: Core primitives for application-level tracing.

packages.name: tracing-log
packages.description: Provides compatibility between `tracing` and the `log` crate.

packages.name: tracing-subscriber
packages.description: Utilities for implementing and composing `tracing` subscribers.

packages.name: typenum
packages.description:
Typenum is a Rust library for type-level numbers evaluated at
    compile time. It currently supports bits, unsigned integers, and signed
    integers. It also provides a type-level array of type-level numbers, but its
    implementation is incomplete.

packages.name: unicode-ident
packages.description: Determine whether characters have the XID_Start or XID_Continue properties according to Unicode Standard Annex #31

packages.name: unicode-segmentation
packages.description:
This crate provides Grapheme Cluster, Word and Sentence boundaries
according to Unicode Standard Annex #29 rules.

packages.name: unicode-width
packages.description:
Determine displayed width of `char` and `str` types
according to Unicode Standard Annex #11 rules.

packages.name: unicode-xid
packages.description:
Determine whether characters have the XID_Start
or XID_Continue properties according to
Unicode Standard Annex #31.

packages.name: universal-hash
packages.description: Traits which describe the functionality of universal hash functions (UHFs)

packages.name: untrusted
packages.description: Safe, fast, zero-panic, zero-crashing, zero-allocation parsing of untrusted inputs in Rust.

packages.name: valuable
packages.description: Object-safe value inspection, used to pass un-typed structured data across trait-object boundaries.

packages.name: version_check
packages.description: Tiny crate to check the version of the installed/running rustc.

packages.name: walkdir
packages.description: Recursively walk a directory.

packages.description: X25519 elliptic curve Diffie-Hellman key exchange in pure-Rust, using curve25519-dalek.

packages.name: zeroize
packages.description:
Securely clear secrets from memory with a simple trait built on
stable Rust primitives which guarantee memory is zeroed using an
operation will not be 'optimized away' by the compiler.
Uses a portable pure Rust implementation that works everywhere,
even WASM!

packages.name: zeroize_derive
packages.description: Custom derive support for zeroize

packages.name: zmij
packages.description: A double-to-string conversion algorithm based on Schubfach and yy
